channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Ireland headed for coalition government following parliamentary election, exit poll suggests
item.description: DUBLIN (AP) — An exit poll in Ireland’s parliamentary election released late Friday suggests the three biggest parties have won roughly equal shares and the country is headed for another coalition government. A poll released as voting ended at 10 p.m.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and PETER MORRISON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: British lawmakers move closer to allowing terminally ill adults to end their lives
item.description: Members of Parliament approved the assisted dying bill by a 330-275 vote, signaling their approval in principle for the bill, which will undergo further scrutiny before it goes to a final vote.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas attacker opens fire at Israeli bus in West Bank, injuring at least 8: report
item.description: Hamas is claiming responsibility Friday for an attack on a bus near an Israeli settlement in the West Bank, which has left at least eight injured, reports say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Customers at this Starbucks can sip coffee and observe a quiet North Korean village
item.description: Coffee drinkers can sip their beverages and view a quiet North Korean mountain village from a new Starbucks at a South Korean border observatory.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: UK lawmakers vote in favor of bill legalizing assisted dying
item.description: Lawmakers in Britain gave initial approval to a bill to allow terminally ill adults to end their lives in England and Wales in a vote in the House of Commons on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Stowaway who boarded New York-to-Paris flight will be sent back to US
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Authorities are investigating after a Russian national who did not have a ticket boarded a Paris-bound Delta flight at New York's John F. Kennedy International Airport this week and was arrested when the plane touched down.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israelis are wary of returning to the north because they don't trust the ceasefire with Hezbollah
item.description: KIBBUTZ MALKIYA, Israel (AP) — Dean Sweetland casts his gaze over a forlorn street in the Israeli community of Kibbutz Malkiya. Perched on a hill overlooking the border with Lebanon, the town stands mostly empty after being abandoned a year ago. The daycare is closed. The homes are unkempt.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK Cabinet member resigns over past phone fraud case
item.description: A member of the U.K. Cabinet has resigned after reports revealed she was convicted in a fraud case more than a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: UK spy chief says Russia waging 'reckless' sabotage campaign in Europe
item.description: The head of Britain's Secret Intelligence Service said Russia's war against Ukraine is part of a “staggeringly reckless campaign of Russian sabotage” aimed at Europe.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Ukrainian energy workers carry out repairs despite Russia's pounding of the country's power grid
item.description: On a bright winter day, workers at a Ukrainian thermal power plant repair its heavily damaged equipment as drops of water from melted snow leak through gaping holes in its battered roof.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bolsonaro floats Trump pressure campaign on Brazil in comeback push
item.description: Former Brazilian President Jair Bolsonaro called on President-elect Trump to help him in his bid to return to power, suggesting Trump in his second term levy economic sanctions on the South American country.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Notre Dame Cathedral unveils its new interior 5 years after devastating fire
item.description: PARIS (AP) — After more than five years of frenetic, but sometimes interrupted, reconstruction work, Notre Dame Cathedral showed itself anew to the world Friday, with rebuilt soaring ceilings and creamy good-as-new stonework erasing somber memories of its devastating fire in 2019.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Georgian protesters clash with police for a second night after EU talks are suspended
item.description: Thousands of demonstrators protesting the Georgian government's decision to suspend negotiations to join the European Union have clashed with police outside the parliament building for a second straight night.
item.dc:creator: Sophiko Megrelidze, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Day 2 of the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire sees Israeli airstrike on Lebanon and scattered attacks
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israel on Thursday carried out its first airstrike on Lebanon since a truce with Hezbollah announced this week, saying it targeted the militant group's activity at a rocket storage facility in southern Lebanon that breached the ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Avocados, tequila and other iconic Mexican products are jeopardized by Trump's tariff threats
item.description: URUAPAN, Mexico (AP) — Mexicans are worried that threats by Donald Trump to impose 25% tariffs could affect a wide range of iconic Mexican products and threaten entire regional economies. In western Mexico, no crop supplies an income for so many small growers as avocados.
item.dc:creator: ARMANDO SOLÍS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian strike on Ukrainian energy grid leaves more than 1M without power
item.description: A Russian strike on energy infrastructure in western Ukraine on Thursday left more than 1 million people without power, according to local officials. Electricity was knocked out for more than 500,000 households in the Lviv region due to the attack, according to regional Gov. Maksym Kozytskyi.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Hiker accidentally uncovers 280-million-year-old footprints in Italy
item.description: Hikers were responsible for the discovery of what scientists believe to be a prehistoric ecosystem. The first sign of the ecosystem discovered by the hikers were footprints.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel opens fire in Lebanon at ‘suspects’ allegedly violating truce, which has entered its second day
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces opened fire Thursday on "suspects" in southern Lebanon who allegedly are violating the terms of the ceasefire deal with Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia launches another large missile, drone attack on Ukraine's energy infrastructure
item.description: Russia launched another attack on Ukraine's energy infrastructure on Thursday, leaving over a million households without power and forcing emergency power to be implemented nationwide.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Space Force looks to bulk up against anti-satellite weapons
item.description: The 5-year-old U.S. Space Force is moving quickly to confront what is becoming its priority challenge: the threat of anti-satellite weapons (ASATs) from foreign adversaries, including Russia and China.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Biden readies $725 million arms aid package for Ukraine: Reuters
item.description: The formal notification to Congress of the weapons package could come as soon as Monday, one official said.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Trump taps retired General Keith Kellogg for Ukraine envoy role
item.description: While there is currently no special envoy for the war in Ukraine, Trump had privately expressed interest in creating the position.
item.dc:creator: Gram Slattery, Svea Herbst-Bayliss and Steve Holland / Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: 2,000-year-old fig unearthed in Ireland marks ‘oldest example of an exotic fruit’ discovered in the area
item.description: During excavations in Ireland, an ancient fig dating back 2,000 years was unearthed. The finding of the fig marks a fist-of-its kind discovery for Ireland.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden team briefed Trump transition on Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire
item.description: President-elect Trump's transition team has received briefings from the chief architect of the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire deal under President Biden, a senior administration official said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: China releases 3 Americans in prisoner swap
item.description: Mark Swidan, Kai Li and John Leung were freed after years of talks between U.S. and Chinese officials.
item.dc:creator: Katie Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pledges Mexico, Canada, China tariffs over border, fentanyl
item.description: "Thousands of people are pouring through Mexico and Canada, bringing Crime and Drugs at levels never seen before," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: 1 dead after DHL cargo plane crashes in Lithuania; officials 'cannot rule out' terrorism
item.description: Lithuanian officials said they were investigating the cause of the crash, and "cannot rule out" terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Siarhei Satsiuk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Drones detected near 3 US bases in England last week: Air Force
item.description: Between Wednesday and Friday, multiple unidentified drones were detected near RAF Lakenheath, RAF Mildenhall and RAF Feltwell.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli strikes in central Beirut kill at least 20 as diplomats push for a cease-fire
item.description: BEIRUT, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli airstrikes Saturday in central Beirut killed at least 20 people, officials said, as the once-rare attacks on the heart of Lebanon's capital continued without warning while diplomats scrambled to broker a cease-fire.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, NewsNation
